name: Faust (Python Stream Processing Library)

auth.notes: Kafka broker credentials via broker URL

performance.notes: Python async stream processing; not Java Kafka Streams level throughput

agent_readiness.notes: Python stream processing with Kafka, inspired by Kafka Streams. import faust. app = faust.App('myapp', broker='kafka://localhost'). topic = app.topic('orders', value_type=Order). @app.agent(topic) async def process(stream): async for order in stream: print(f'Order {order.id}'). @app.timer(interval=10.0) async def every_10_seconds(): await do_work(). Tables: user_table = app.Table('user_scores', default=int). user_table['user1'] += 10 (changelog-backed). Windowing: user_table.tumbling(60).relative_to_now(). Schemas: class Order(faust.Record): id: int; amount: float. Serialization: JSON, msgpack, protobuf. Workers: faust worker -A myapp. Web: built-in aiohttp server for admin/monitoring. Faust-streaming is maintained fork of original Robinhood Faust.

gotchas: Original Robinhood Faust is abandoned — use faust-streaming fork
Python GIL limits throughput vs Java Kafka Streams
Table windowing is complex to reason about
Requires Kafka or RabbitMQ as broker
faust-streaming is community-maintained — slower release cadence
best_when: Python teams needing Kafka Streams-like stream processing
Real-time event processing where Python ecosystem is required
Microservices needing async Kafka consumers with business logic
avoid_when: High-throughput data pipelines (use Java Kafka Streams or Flink)
When simple Kafka consumption without state is needed (use aiokafka)
Teams unfamiliar with stream processing concepts
summary: Faust (faust-streaming) is a Python Kafka stream processing library. app = faust.App('myapp', broker='kafka://...'); @app.agent(topic) async def process(stream): async for event in stream. Stateful Tables with windowing. Built-in web admin. JSON/msgpack/protobuf schemas. BSD-3-Clause. Use faust-streaming fork (original Robinhood Faust abandoned). Python GIL limits throughput vs Java Kafka Streams. Best for Python-native stateful Kafka stream processing.
